channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ex-DOJ official goes public with blistering criticism of his former bosses
item.description: Until recently, Jonathan Gross was a Trump political appointee at the Department of Justice and worked on its "Weaponization Working Group." He has now become a vocal critic of the department.
item.content:encoded: Until recently, Jonathan Gross was a Trump political appointee at the Department of Justice and worked on its "Weaponization Working Group." He has now become a vocal critic of the department.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
item.description: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Voters are caught in the middle as the redistricting battle intensifies
item.description: Much of the focus of the ongoing redistricting war has been on which political party will come out on top. But it's voters who will pay a cost, say voting experts and voting rights advocates.
item.content:encoded:
Much of the focus of the ongoing redistricting war has been on which political party will come out on top. But it's voters who will pay a cost, say voting experts and voting rights advocates.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping for summit during first day in the country, what Asia thinks of Trump's visit, appeals court hears arguments from law firms targeted by Trump.
item.content:encoded: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping for summit during first day in the country, what Asia thinks of Trump's visit, appeals court hears arguments from law firms targeted by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: How Asia is reacting to Trump's summit in China
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing for his big summit with Chinese leaders. How Asia is reacting to the summit.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is in Beijing for his big summit with Chinese leaders. How Asia is reacting to the summit.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: UAE denies Netanyahu secretly visited the country during the Iran war
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu quietly visited the United Arab Emirates during the Israeli-U.S. war with Iran, his office said Wednesday. The UAE later denied any secret visit had occurred.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu quietly visited the United Arab Emirates during the Israeli-U.S. war with Iran, his office said Wednesday. The UAE later denied any secret visit had occurred.

(Image credit: Ilia Yefimovich/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1 and injuring 31
item.description: Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.
item.content:encoded:
Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.

(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
item.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.
item.content:encoded:
Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

(Image credit: Rebecca S. Gratz)
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow

item.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
item.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
item.content:encoded:
Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
